126851.explanation: Ideally this is the case, but practically not always possible.

126854.explanation: We stimulate this through the energy coach.

126856.explanation: This should not be the municipality’s responsibility, but the entrepreneurs’ own.

126860.explanation: Our municipality is too extensive for that.

126864.explanation: Provided it fits within the Vital Countryside vision.

126867.explanation: This is the responsibility of parents and schools.

126870.explanation: No, but if insecurity increases, enforcement must be intensified immediately.

126871.explanation: There is currently no greater need for it.

126878.explanation: However, this is situational and varies per issue.

126879.explanation: This income threshold is already at a sufficient level and was adjusted upwards a few years ago.

126882.explanation: This is desirable to increase safety.

126883.explanation: However, this is up to the care recipient themselves.

126884.explanation: However, quality is of greater importance.

126886.explanation: Currently, there is a prevention policy, which is already fairly strict with unrecognizable municipal enforcement officers (BOAs). If signs of alcohol use among youth increase, stricter enforcement will be necessary.

126887.explanation: This mainly occurs in the Maaskant area; however, it must always be viewed in proportion.

126893.explanation: This is not a national policy.

126896.explanation: The responsibility lies with the parents. We recognize that every child must learn to swim; however, this does not have to be through school swimming lessons. It would also mean this must take place in Druten, which leads to logistical challenges and high costs.

126897.explanation: This fits within cultural policy, but it is up to the schools themselves to respond. Current cultural initiatives are already subsidized.

126900.explanation: This affects the landscape too much.

126901.explanation: Currently in order, certainly not less.

126849.explanation: However, quality takes precedence over quantity.

126851.explanation: Ideally this is the case, but practically not always possible.

126871.explanation: There is currently no greater need for it.

126878.explanation: However, this is situational and varies per issue.

126882.explanation: This is desirable to increase safety.

126883.explanation: However, this is up to the care recipient themselves.

126884.explanation: However, quality is of greater importance.

126886.explanation: Currently, there is a prevention policy, which is already fairly strict with unrecognizable municipal enforcement officers (BOAs). If signs of alcohol use among youth increase, stricter enforcement will be necessary.

126887.explanation: This mainly occurs in the Maaskant area; however, it must always be viewed in proportion.

126893.explanation: This is not a national policy.

126896.explanation: The responsibility lies with the parents. We recognize that every child must learn to swim; however, this does not have to be through school swimming lessons. It would also mean this must take place in Druten, which leads to logistical challenges and high costs.

126897.explanation: This fits within cultural policy, but it is up to the schools themselves to respond. Current cultural initiatives are already subsidized.

126900.explanation: This affects the landscape too much.

126901.explanation: Currently in order, certainly not less.

126849.explanation: The VVD believes that quality and suitability should be decisive in forming the council. Diversity is important, but fixed quotas for men and women should never be more important than placing the right person in the right position.

126851.explanation: The VVD is positive about initiatives where residents take responsibility for their living environment. Self-management can enhance community spirit and involvement. However, it must be voluntary and appropriate for the situation; the municipality remains responsible for safety and quality.

126854.explanation: The VVD is positive about residents who want to jointly generate sustainable energy, provided it fits the environment. The municipality can support by thinking along and removing obstacles. Structural financial support is not a given and requires careful consideration.

126856.explanation: The VVD believes it is important that agricultural entrepreneurs can properly house their international workers. Housing on their own company premises can help, provided this is done within clear municipal rules and with attention to livability, environment, and safety.

126860.explanation: The VVD believes the municipality should not organize a fireworks show itself. The municipality should be positive towards initiatives from entrepreneurs and neighborhoods and cooperate within clear frameworks. Safety, local support, and clear agreements are paramount.

126864.explanation: The VVD is cautious about housing construction in rural areas. We prefer to build new homes within village boundaries to protect landscape and livability. Therefore, strict frameworks apply for building on agricultural land, and it is not automatic. Infill before expansion!

126867.explanation: The VVD values music education but sees large differences in availability and costs. Good opportunities already exist through associations and providers. We choose to stimulate music education selectively where needed, rather than subsidizing every primary school child by default.

126870.explanation: The VVD believes that additional enforcement officers are needed for a safe and livable municipality. More 'blue' BOAs strengthen supervision and visibility in villages, while 'green' BOAs are important for rural areas, nature, and agricultural surroundings. This way we increase safety and enforcement.

126871.explanation: The VVD is open to camera surveillance where it contributes to safety and tackling nuisance. It is not an end in itself and not needed everywhere. Camera surveillance should be used purposefully, with attention to privacy and effectiveness.

126873.explanation: The VVD believes local ties should be taken into account when allocating social rental housing. Residents of West Maas and Waal should have a fair chance to remain living in their own municipality and therefore have priority in allocation.

126878.explanation: The VVD finds it important to help residents with financial problems. Forgiveness of debts may be necessary but does not always have to be full. We consider it reasonable that, where possible, a personal contribution is requested, with consideration for individual circumstances.

126879.explanation: The VVD finds it important that residents who need support receive it. The current income threshold and arrangements offer sufficient room for this. We should focus on properly utilizing existing assistance, with attention to those in financial difficulty.

126882.explanation: The VVD values traffic safety in our villages. A maximum speed of 30 km/h can help, but not automatically everywhere. Each street must be evaluated for what is needed. Rules only make sense if they are understandable and enforceable.

126883.explanation: The VVD believes good and accessible care must always come first. Considering someone’s background is important, but the municipality cannot always guarantee customized care for every request. Inclusivity is important but also has limits within available care and resources.

126884.explanation: The VVD believes that youth care should not primarily focus on the lowest price. Good and appropriate help for young people is paramount. However, the municipality must steer more sharply on effectiveness and costs, so care remains affordable and children receive the right support.

126886.explanation: The VVD thinks it is important to prevent alcohol use among youth. This requires collaboration between parents, sports clubs, and the municipality. Enforcement should be within legal frameworks, but prevention and shared responsibility work better than solely strict intervention.

126887.explanation: Large holiday parks are not a given due to their impact on nature, landscape, and the livability of surrounding villages. At the same time, they can contribute to the local economy and offer opportunities for entrepreneurs. Safe and peaceful living remains an important principle for the VVD.

126888.explanation: The VVD finds it important to handle the living environment and health carefully. Many farmers are economically dependent on their businesses. We do not consider a generic reduction in pig numbers self-evident; tailor-made and future-oriented choices per farm are needed.

126893.explanation: The VVD understands that refugees with residence permits need help to build a new life here. At the same time, we find it important that our residents are treated fairly and do not structurally come last in the allocation of social rental housing.

126895.explanation: The VVD wants to carefully manage our landscape and agricultural land. Solar panels should preferably be placed on the roofs of homes, businesses, and barns, where ample space remains. Large-scale use of agricultural land is not obvious to us and requires tailor-made solutions.

126896.explanation: The VVD finds it important that children learn to swim well. In a watery municipality like West Maas and Waal, this is essential for safety. School swimming can play an important role and, in our view, should once again be a regular part of primary education.

126897.explanation: The VVD appreciates if schools pay attention to our Maos and Waol languages but does not see this as a municipal obligation. Schools should be able to make their own choices about this. When schools take initiatives, the municipality should be open to them.

126900.explanation: The VVD is against building high residential flats in West Maas and Waal. Our municipality has an open, rural character with small-scale villages. High-rise buildings do not fit the landscape and village image we want to preserve.

126901.explanation: The VVD opposes reducing parking spaces in the villages. Good accessibility is essential for residents, shops, and services. Especially in Beneden-Leeuwen, but also in Dreumel, where many facilities come together, sufficient parking spaces are needed for livability and accessibility.

126849.explanation: However, quality takes precedence over quantity.

126851.explanation: Ideally this is the case, but practically not always possible.

126854.explanation: We stimulate this through the energy coach.

126856.explanation: This is the responsibility of the entrepreneurs themselves.

126860.explanation: Our municipality is too extensive for that.

126864.explanation: Provided it fits within the Vital Countryside vision.

126867.explanation: This is the responsibility of parents and schools.

126870.explanation: No, but if insecurity increases, enforcement must be intensified immediately.

126871.explanation: There is currently no need for this.

126878.explanation: However, this is situational and varies per issue.

126879.explanation: This income threshold is already at a sufficient level and was adjusted upwards a few years ago.

126882.explanation: This is desirable to increase safety.

126883.explanation: However, this is up to the care recipient themselves.

126884.explanation: However, quality is of greater importance.

126886.explanation: Currently, there is a prevention policy, which is already fairly strict with unrecognizable municipal enforcement officers (BOAs). If signs of alcohol use among youth increase, stricter enforcement will be necessary.

126887.explanation: This mainly occurs in the Maaskant area; however, it must always be viewed in proportion.

126888.explanation: There is no policy for this, so as far as we are concerned it is not needed now.

126893.explanation: This is not a national policy.

126896.explanation: The responsibility lies with the parents. We recognize that every child must learn to swim; however, this does not have to be through school swimming lessons. It would also mean this must take place in Druten, which leads to logistical challenges and high costs.

126897.explanation: This fits within cultural policy, but it is up to the schools themselves to respond. Current cultural initiatives are already subsidized.

126900.explanation: This affects the landscape too much.

126901.explanation: Currently in order, certainly not less.

126849.explanation: According to SMW, it should always be about the quality of the people. Three women is also fine!

126851.explanation: Never fully, as the municipality is ultimately responsible.

126854.explanation: Sustainability must be supported, possibly also financially.

126856.explanation: This is possible, but central reception locations can also be established.

126860.explanation: Municipalities must set a good example, so polluting the air, soil, and water just for fun is no longer acceptable.

126864.explanation: Housing construction requires vision and a good layout of public space. You cannot just randomly build houses anywhere.

126870.explanation: It is good that the public enforcement officers (boa's) become more visible, not only to enforce rules but also to engage in conversations with residents to explain how we must work together for a pleasant society.

126871.explanation: Cameras do not solve anything; we must converse with our residents about what is going wrong and ask them what we can do together about it.

126873.explanation:
The housing association operates with a waiting list, so those who have been on the list the longest are served first. 

Also people from outside our municipality. This seems like a good system to me. So not local residents first, but whoever is highest on the waiting list first. 

Urgent applicants from our own municipality currently have priority, so in that case, local residents go first. 

The long waiting lists will only be resolved if more social housing is built. 

In WMW (West Maas en Waal), the highest percentage of construction in the Netherlands has taken place. 

However, regarding social housing, we have been at only 14% over the past 4 years. 

According to the agreements in the housing vision, it should be 30%, so in this regard WMW falls seriously short.

126878.explanation: There is no point in pushing people into debt. Debt forgiveness only occurs if income is insufficient.

126879.explanation: There is currently sufficient financial support. Support on a personal level can still be expanded, for example through coaching on how to manage your budget and not forgetting to learn how to declutter.

126882.explanation: This is already the case, but enforcement is lacking. As for road layout, various measures can still be taken. Think of bike lanes, bicycle streets, and pedestrian crossings.

126883.explanation: The municipality has contracts with many care providers, allowing for a choice. The choice is not unlimited. Everything must remain feasible and affordable. The most important thing is that care is provided.

126884.explanation: The starting point must be to provide the right care.

126886.explanation:
Strict enforcement is not the right wording. In consultation with administrations, appropriate measures must be taken. Administrations must recognize the necessity of the measures. Attention must also be paid to the example that adults set for the youth. 

Indeed, harsh enforcement usually backfires, especially with youth. 
Focus on raising awareness among clubs, parents, and young people.

126887.explanation: With over 160,000 overnight stays per year, there is no room for large-scale holiday parks in our opinion. Therefore, we should focus on small-scale facilities such as bed and breakfasts and mini-campsites.

126888.explanation: We must get rid of intensive livestock farming, which is better for animal welfare and for our air, water, and soil quality. Farmers must become co-managers of nature. A financial scheme should be established for this. Organic farming must be rewarding.

126893.explanation: This is regulated by law; every municipality must annually accommodate a fixed number of refugees with residence permits.

126895.explanation: Land is scarce in our densely populated country. There are enough roofs where solar panels can still be installed.

126896.explanation: We also allocate funds for other sports and music lessons, so why not for school swimming? We should cover the swimming pool in Dreumel.

126897.explanation: Teachers already have more than enough on their plates.

126900.explanation: We too must use public space as efficiently as possible; three residential floors is perfectly fine.

126901.explanation: We want to green the villages in the context of climate adaptation. Paved parking spaces will have to give way to more greenery. We will need to do our small shopping more often on foot or by bike. This is excellent for promoting healthy behavior.

126849.explanation: The quality of executive members is decisive for the CDA, regardless of gender.

126851.explanation: The CDA encourages the idea of having residents manage, for example, public parks and buildings. Co-ownership is valuable, but good agreements must underpin this. It must not be the case that residents risk problems through management responsibilities.

126854.explanation: The CDA wants to support every green initiative set up by a group of residents. Through this, we can work towards a climate-resilient municipality by 2035.

126856.explanation: In the past council period, the Action Plan for International Workers in West Maas en Waal was adopted. This was developed together with various interest groups and entrepreneurs. The CDA wants to continue following this policy for continuity and clarity.

126860.explanation: A national fireworks ban is now in effect. One alternative the CDA sees is the possibility to organize fireworks displays at various locations. This depends on how national policy develops.

126864.explanation: It is already possible within the policy frameworks of the VAB policy to build houses on released land. The CDA wants to continue following this policy, so that a controlled number of homes are built in rural areas and no mass housing occurs.

126867.explanation: The CDA finds it too much to subsidize music lessons for every child. We support directing subsidies based on need. If a primary school child has a need for music lessons, there should be room for a subsidy.

126870.explanation: The CDA stands for a safe living environment. We want more public enforcement officers (boa's) for manageable areas. This makes their work feasible and makes them more recognizable to our residents.

126871.explanation: Where necessary, the CDA supports increased camera surveillance. This prevents nuisance, damage, and insecurity.

126873.explanation: There are many housing seekers within West Maas en Waal. The CDA believes that those originating from our municipality, and therefore having a socio-economic connection to the area, should be given priority for social rental housing.

126878.explanation: The CDA wants to protect the vulnerable in society, including those with low incomes. Therefore, we do not think it wise to stop debt forgiveness.

126879.explanation: The CDA believes that the income threshold should increase in line with inflation. This ensures that those in need receive the necessary support and fewer people fall through the cracks.

126882.explanation: In terms of traffic safety, the CDA believes the maximum speed in built-up areas should be 30 km/h. The primary responsibility for adhering to the speed limit lies with the driver. We want the municipality to actively implement policy on this through a campaign.

126883.explanation: The CDA stands for individual freedom of choice and supports free choice of healthcare provider.

126884.explanation: The CDA advocates for good healthcare. Quality is paramount. People must receive good care where the quality of the care provided takes precedence, not the final bill.

126886.explanation: The CDA recognizes that many young people consume alcohol. However, we believe it is the parents' responsibility to discuss this with their children. The same applies to sports canteens. Responsibility lies with the clubs and bar staff to supervise this.

126887.explanation: West Maas en Waal is a tourist area. The CDA supports the tourism sector and the entrepreneurs connected to it. We disagree with withholding permits upfront. With every application, tailored solutions are better in the CDA's view.

126888.explanation: The CDA believes it is not the municipality's role to reduce the number of pigs. The agricultural sector can play a role in this itself. Ultimately, it is up to each individual farmer to decide within the applicable permits and laws.

126893.explanation: There are many housing seekers within West Maas en Waal. The CDA believes that those originating from our municipality, and therefore having a socio-economic connection to the area, should be given priority for social rental housing.

126895.explanation: The municipality currently meets the targets set by the Regional Energy Strategy. Solar fields have already been installed on agricultural lands. The CDA believes the municipality has sufficient solar fields and that valuable farmland should no longer be lost.

126896.explanation: As far as the CDA is concerned, swimming lessons should be accessible to everyone, including those with limited financial means. If school swimming can contribute to this, we see it as a good measure.

126897.explanation: Maas and Waal has a unique culture and dialect. The CDA believes this cultural heritage must be preserved for future generations. By addressing it in schools, we keep the Maos and Waols alive.

126900.explanation: The CDA supports housing initiatives with multiple floors. It is a solution for the housing shortage and can offer opportunities for starters and seniors. Good policy on numbers and locations is necessary.

126901.explanation: In a rural municipality like West Maas en Waal, residents depend on cars. Therefore, the CDA does not want to reduce the number of parking spaces because we do not want to affect the accessibility and mobility of our area.

126851.explanation: Ideally this is the case, but practically not always possible.

126854.explanation: We stimulate this through the energy coach.

126856.explanation: This should not be the municipality’s responsibility, but the entrepreneurs’ own.

126860.explanation: Our municipality is too extensive for that.

126864.explanation: Provided it fits within the Vital Countryside vision.

126867.explanation: This is the responsibility of parents and schools.

126870.explanation: No, but if insecurity increases, enforcement must be intensified immediately.

126871.explanation: There is currently no greater need for it.

126878.explanation: This depends on the situation and the issue.

126879.explanation: This income threshold is already at a sufficient level and was adjusted upwards a few years ago.

126882.explanation: This is desirable to increase safety.

126883.explanation: However, this is up to the care recipient themselves.

126884.explanation: However, quality is of greater importance.

126886.explanation: Currently, there is a prevention policy, which is already fairly strict with unrecognizable municipal enforcement officers (BOAs). If signs of alcohol use among youth increase, stricter enforcement will be necessary.

126887.explanation: This mainly occurs in the Maaskant area; however, it must always be viewed in proportion.

126888.explanation: Farmers are given the space to develop their business. There is a maximum per location.

126893.explanation: This is not a national policy.

126896.explanation: The responsibility lies with the parents. We recognize that every child must learn to swim; however, this does not have to be through school swimming lessons. It would also mean this must take place in Druten, which leads to logistical challenges and high costs.

126897.explanation: This fits within cultural policy, but it is up to the schools themselves to respond. Current cultural initiatives are already subsidized.

126900.explanation: Apartments with 3 to 4 floors are possible.

126901.explanation: Currently in order, certainly not less.

126849.explanation: However, quality is more important than quantity and there must be sufficient candidates.

126851.explanation: It would be nice if a situation allows it, but it should not be the starting point.

126854.explanation: We support residents through the energy coach.

126856.explanation: Entrepreneurs must take their own responsibility. It should not be placed on the municipality.

126860.explanation: The municipality should not take this up. Our municipality is too spread out for this.

126864.explanation: This can and may only be done if it fits within our Vital Countryside Vision.

126867.explanation: Parents and schools are responsible for their music lessons. The municipality supports this through regional agreements where possible.

126870.explanation: The municipality is largely safe, but if insecurity increases, intensification may be necessary.

126871.explanation: It is not desirable to deploy more camera surveillance without good reason.

126873.explanation: It is certainly desirable to be able to give priority to our own residents.

126878.explanation: The municipality should not routinely waive all municipal taxes but apply this where necessary, as regulated in our ordinance.

126879.explanation: The income threshold was recently raised and is at a sufficient level.

126882.explanation: It is probably already regulated everywhere, but where it is not yet regulated, it should be adjusted.

126883.explanation: Basically, the care recipient should be able to make their own choice.

126884.explanation: When coordinating contracts, price is certainly important, but the quality of care must come first.

126886.explanation: Our current prevention policy already focuses on this, and if alcohol use increases, stricter enforcement may occur.

126887.explanation: Recreational enhancement can be used where possible, but in consultation with our residents and fitting the environment.

126893.explanation: Every resident must be treated equally.

126895.explanation: The municipality should certainly discourage it.

126896.explanation: The primary responsibility lies with the parents. We acknowledge it is necessary that every child learns to swim. However, this does not necessarily have to be through school swimming.

126897.explanation: It is up to the schools themselves to handle this. Within the current cultural policy, many initiatives are possible.

126900.explanation: This does not fit in our living environment.

126901.explanation: There must be sufficient parking spaces in the centers. Certainly not fewer.

statements.title: The municipality should reduce the number of pigs. There are currently 100,000.
West Maas and Waal should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.text: In West Maas en Waal moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: West Maas and Waal should allow more high-rise residential blocks.
There should be fewer parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten minder parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
West Maas and Waal should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with safety.

titleParts.text: In West Maas en Waal moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should take strong action against alcohol use by young people in sports canteens.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to Maos and Waols.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should not grant permission for the construction of large holiday parks.

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

titleParts.text: geven voor de bouw van grote vakantieparken.
